all.en: Saturday morning began with Dad announcing his ambitious plan to clean out the attic.
all.zh: 星期六早晨，爸爸宣布了他清理閣樓的雄心計畫。

all.en: The whole family groaned, but everyone knew they couldn't escape this dusty mission.
all.zh: 全家人一起哀號，但大家都知道逃不了這場灰塵任務。

all.en: Mom climbed the wooden ladder first, sneezing the moment she opened the trapdoor.
all.zh: 媽媽先爬上木梯，一打開閣樓門就忍不住打了個噴嚏。

all.en: Boxes of old photographs, broken toys, and forgotten clothes filled every corner.
all.zh: 舊照片、壞掉的玩具和被遺忘的衣服堆滿了每個角落。

all.en: Sarah discovered her childhood diary and immediately sat down to read it.
all.zh: 莎拉發現了她小時候的日記，立刻坐下來翻閱。

all.en: "Hey, no slacking off!" her brother Tom shouted while carrying a heavy box downstairs.
all.zh: 「喂，不准偷懶！」她哥哥湯姆扛著一個重箱子下樓時喊道。

all.en: They created three piles: keep, donate, and throw away, but the first pile grew the fastest.
all.zh: 他們分成三堆：保留、捐贈和丟棄，但保留那一堆卻長得最快。

all.en: Dad found his old electric guitar and started playing a terribly out-of-tune song.
all.zh: 爸爸找到了他的舊電吉他，開始彈一首走音得很離譜的歌。

all.en: Mom rolled her eyes and reminded him that they were supposed to be working.
all.zh: 媽媽翻了個白眼，提醒他他們應該在工作才對。

all.en: By lunchtime, they had filled six bags for donation and uncovered countless memories.
all.zh: 到午餐時，他們已經裝了六袋要捐贈的東西，也挖出了無數的回憶。

all.en: They ordered pizza and ate it sitting on the attic floor, surrounded by their past.
all.zh: 他們訂了披薩，坐在閣樓地板上吃著，被自己的過往環繞著。

all.en: Tom admitted that this was actually the most fun he'd had with his family in months.
all.zh: 湯姆承認這其實是他幾個月來和家人最開心的一次了。

all.en: After lunch, Sarah found a dusty wedding album from her parents' early years.
all.zh: 午餐後，莎拉找到了一本父母年輕時佈滿灰塵的婚禮相簿。

all.en: Mom blushed when the kids pointed at her huge eighties hairstyle in the photos.
all.zh: 當孩子們指著照片裡她巨大的八零年代髮型時，媽媽臉紅了。

all.en: Dad insisted that his old guitar was still valuable and refused to put it in the donation pile.
all.zh: 爸爸堅持他的舊吉他還很有價值，拒絕把它放進捐贈那堆。

all.en: Tom suggested they hold a small garage sale next weekend to make some extra money.
all.zh: 湯姆建議下週末他們辦一場小型的車庫拍賣賺點外快。

all.en: Everyone agreed, and Sarah started designing colorful price tags on her tablet.
all.zh: 大家都同意了，莎拉開始用平板設計色彩繽紛的價格標籤。

all.en: By late afternoon, the attic looked twice as big and much easier to walk around in.
all.zh: 到了傍晚，閣樓看起來大了兩倍，走動也輕鬆許多。

all.en: They installed a new ceiling light so the space wouldn't feel so gloomy anymore.
all.zh: 他們裝了一盞新的吸頂燈，這樣空間就不會再那麼陰暗。

all.en: Mom proposed turning the empty corner into a small reading nook for the family.
all.zh: 媽媽提議把空出來的角落改造成全家的小型閱讀角。

all.en: Tom carried up an old armchair, while Sarah added cushions and a soft lamp.
all.zh: 湯姆扛上一張舊扶手椅，莎拉則擺上抱枕和一盞柔和的檯燈。

all.en: That evening, Dad sat in the new reading corner and quietly admitted he loved how the attic looked.
all.zh: 那晚，爸爸坐進新的閱讀角，悄悄承認他很喜歡閣樓現在的樣子。

all.en: The following weekend, Tom and Sarah held the small garage sale they had planned together.
all.zh: 下個週末，湯姆和莎拉一起辦了他們先前計畫好的小型車庫拍賣。

all.en: Neighbors came by all morning, surprised at how many treasures the family had stored above their heads.
all.zh: 整個上午鄰居陸續上門，驚訝這家人竟在頭頂上方藏了這麼多寶物。

all.en: One older gentleman offered Dad a fair price for the dusty electric guitar from the eighties.
all.zh: 一位年長的紳士願意以合理的價錢買下那把八零年代的舊電吉他。

all.en: Dad hesitated for a long moment, but finally smiled and agreed to let the instrument go.
all.zh: 爸爸猶豫了好一會兒，最後笑著答應讓那把樂器離開。

all.en: By the end of the sale, they had earned enough money for a small family dinner downtown.
all.zh: 拍賣結束時，他們賺到的錢足以到市區享用一頓小型的家庭晚餐。

all.en: Sarah suggested they spend part of the profit on a new soft rug for the reading nook.
all.zh: 莎拉建議拿一部分收益去買一張新的軟地毯，放在閱讀角。

all.en: They visited a nearby furniture shop the next day and picked a warm cream-colored rug together.
all.zh: 隔天他們去了附近的家具行，一起挑了一張溫暖的米白色地毯。

all.en: Mom added a small wooden side table for her tea, completing the cozy corner she had imagined.
all.zh: 媽媽加上一張小木邊桌放茶杯，完成了她心中那個溫馨角落的構想。

all.en: Tom surprised everyone by spending each evening reading novels in the attic instead of gaming.
all.zh: 湯姆讓大家驚訝的是，他每晚都待在閣樓看小說，而不是打電動。

all.en: The attic, once dark and forgotten, had truly become the heart of their family weekends.
all.zh: 曾經陰暗被遺忘的閣樓，如今真正成了他們週末家庭生活的核心。

all.en: As autumn arrived, the family decided to install a small wooden bookshelf along one attic wall.
all.zh: 秋天到來時，全家決定在閣樓一面牆裝上一個小型木製書櫃。

all.en: Sarah carefully organized the books by genre and even labeled each shelf with handwritten tags.
all.zh: 莎拉細心地按照類別整理書籍，還親手寫了標籤貼在每一層書架上。

all.en: Tom donated half of his old comic books, claiming he had finally outgrown them at age sixteen.
all.zh: 湯姆捐出了他一半的舊漫畫，宣稱十六歲的自己終於把它們看膩了。

all.en: Mom hung soft fairy lights around the corner to make the reading nook feel even more magical.
all.zh: 媽媽在角落掛上柔和的小燈串，讓閱讀角變得更夢幻。

all.en: Dad surprised everyone by bringing up an old record player he had repaired in secret.
all.zh: 爸爸出其不意地搬上一台他偷偷修好的舊式唱盤機。

all.en: They played a soft jazz album, and the music made the attic feel like a cozy little cafe.
all.zh: 他們放了一張柔和的爵士唱片，音樂讓閣樓宛如一間溫馨的小咖啡館。

all.en: Sarah invited her best friend over to study, and the two girls spent hours reading by the window.
all.zh: 莎拉邀請她最好的朋友來讀書，兩個女孩在窗邊一讀就是好幾個小時。

all.en: Tom built a small ladder from leftover wood so the lower shelves would be easier to reach.
all.zh: 湯姆用剩下的木材做了一個小梯子，方便拿取較低層的書。

all.en: On rainy afternoons, the whole family naturally gathered in the attic with cups of warm tea.
all.zh: 下雨的午後，全家自然而然地聚到閣樓，手裡捧著一杯杯熱茶。

all.en: Mom realized that one weekend of cleaning had quietly transformed the entire shape of family life.
all.zh: 媽媽體會到一個週末的整理，悄悄改變了整個家庭生活的樣貌。

all.en: By winter, the attic had become so popular that Tom suggested installing a small electric heater.
all.zh: 到了冬天，閣樓變得太受歡迎，湯姆建議再裝一台小型電暖器。

all.en: Sarah baked warm cinnamon cookies and brought them upstairs to share during snowy afternoons.
all.zh: 莎拉烤了溫熱的肉桂餅乾，下雪的午後端到樓上和大家分享。

all.en: Dad rotated the jazz records each week, slowly building a small collection on the new bookshelf.
all.zh: 爸爸每週更換爵士唱片，慢慢在新書櫃旁累積出一個小型收藏。

all.en: Mom invited two of her closest friends over and they gathered for tea under the soft fairy lights.
all.zh: 媽媽邀請了兩位最好的朋友過來，大家在柔和的小燈串下圍坐喝茶。

all.en: They admired the cozy reading nook and asked Mom to help redecorate their own attics next spring.
all.zh: 她們欣賞這個溫馨的閱讀角，請媽媽明年春天幫忙改造她們各自家中的閣樓。

all.en: Tom started writing his first short story up there, claiming the silence helped him focus deeply.
all.zh: 湯姆開始在樓上寫他的第一篇短篇小說，宣稱那份寧靜能幫助他深度專注。

all.en: Sarah set up a small easel near the window and began painting quiet winter landscapes outside.
all.zh: 莎拉在窗邊架起一個小畫架，開始畫窗外寧靜的冬季景色。

all.en: Dad surprised everyone by hanging one of Sarah's paintings above the wooden bookshelf in the attic.
all.zh: 爸爸出其不意地把莎拉的一幅畫掛在閣樓的木製書櫃上方。

all.en: Mom hosted a small family ceremony to name the attic "The Lantern Room" because of the warm lights.
all.zh: 媽媽舉辦了一場小型家庭儀式，因為那些溫暖的燈光，將閣樓取名為「燈籠之間」。

all.en: That night, the whole family sat together in silence, listening to soft music and watching the snow fall.
all.zh: 那晚，全家人靜靜地坐在一起，聽著輕柔的音樂，看著窗外的雪緩緩飄落。

all.en: Throughout the winter, the family gathered in the Lantern Room every Sunday evening without fail.
all.zh: 整個冬天，全家每個週日晚上都會準時聚在燈籠房裡。

all.en: Sarah painted a new winter landscape each month, and Dad displayed every finished piece proudly.
all.zh: 莎拉每個月都會畫一幅新的冬景，而爸爸總是驕傲地把完成的作品展示出來。

all.en: Mom began reading aloud from a thick novel, one short chapter beside the soft yellow lantern light.
all.zh: 媽媽開始大聲朗讀一本厚厚的小說，在柔和的黃色燈光旁每次只念一個短章節。

all.en: Sarah's younger brother joined the evenings, bringing his sketchbook to draw quiet portraits of everyone.
all.zh: 莎拉的弟弟也加入了這些夜晚，帶著素描本悄悄為每個人畫安靜的肖像。

all.en: One stormy Sunday, the power went out, but the lanterns kept the Lantern Room warm and bright.
all.zh: 某個暴風雨的週日晚上停電了，但燈籠仍讓燈籠房保持溫暖明亮。

all.en: Dad told an old childhood ghost story, and Sarah was the only one brave enough not to scream.
all.zh: 爸爸講了一個小時候的鬼故事，只有莎拉勇敢到沒有尖叫出聲。

all.en: When spring approached, the family began planning a small open house to share the attic with neighbors.
all.zh: 春天接近時，全家開始計畫舉辦一場小型開放日，把閣樓分享給鄰居。

all.en: Mom baked dozens of cookies, and Sarah hung a row of her favorite paintings along the slanted wall.
all.zh: 媽媽烤了好幾打餅乾，莎拉沿著斜牆掛了一排她最喜歡的畫作。

all.en: On the open house day, twelve neighbors crowded inside, admiring the lanterns and the soft attic glow.
all.zh: 開放日當天，十二位鄰居擠進閣樓，欣賞著燈籠和那柔和的閣樓光暈。

all.en: As the last guest left, Sarah whispered that the Lantern Room had truly become the heart of their home.
all.zh: 最後一位客人離開時，莎拉輕聲說燈籠房真的已經成為他們家的核心。

all.en: Word of the cozy attic spread through the neighborhood, and three families soon asked Mom for design advice.
all.zh: 閣樓的溫馨故事傳遍鄰里，很快就有三戶人家來向媽媽請教設計建議。

all.en: Mom happily accepted the requests, treating them as a small new hobby rather than a serious business.
all.zh: 媽媽欣然接受這些邀請，把它當成一項小小的新嗜好，而不是正式的事業。

all.en: Sarah began teaching a free painting class for two children who lived in the apartment next door.
all.zh: 莎拉開始為住在隔壁公寓的兩個孩子免費教畫畫課。

all.en: Tom finished his first short story and shyly read it aloud to the family in the Lantern Room.
all.zh: 湯姆完成了他第一篇短篇小說，害羞地在燈籠房裡讀給家人聽。

all.en: Everyone clapped warmly, and Dad insisted they save a printed copy among the books on the bookshelf.
all.zh: 大家熱烈地拍手，爸爸堅持要把列印出的稿子留在書櫃上的書群裡保存。

all.en: By early summer, the family had added a small skylight, allowing fresh sunshine into the attic every morning.
all.zh: 初夏時節，全家又裝了一扇小天窗，讓清晨的陽光每天都能灑進閣樓。

all.en: Sarah painted a sunrise scene directly under the skylight to celebrate the new flow of natural light.
all.zh: 莎拉直接在天窗下方畫了一幅日出景象，慶祝這道新引入的自然光。

all.en: Mom started a notebook recording every visitor and which lantern they chose to sit beside that evening.
all.zh: 媽媽開了一本筆記本，記錄每位來訪的客人以及他們當晚選擇坐在哪盞燈籠旁。

all.en: Tom suggested they host an outdoor garden tea party to thank the neighbors for their year of friendship.
all.zh: 湯姆建議他們在花園舉辦一場戶外茶會，感謝鄰居們這一年來的情誼。

all.en: That night under the lanterns, the family agreed the cluttered attic had quietly given them a new beginning.
all.zh: 那晚在燈籠下，全家一致同意這個曾經雜亂的閣樓悄悄帶給他們一個全新的開始。

all.en: The garden tea party drew almost twenty neighbors, who wandered between the lawn and the attic all afternoon.
all.zh: 花園茶會吸引了將近二十位鄰居，他們整個下午在草坪和閣樓之間穿梭。

all.en: Sarah painted small thank-you cards by hand and gave one personally to every guest at the gate.
all.zh: 莎拉親手畫了一張張小小的感謝卡，在門口親自送給每位客人。

all.en: Tom read a short passage from his new story while everyone listened politely under the apple tree.
all.zh: 湯姆在蘋果樹下朗讀他新故事的一段，大家都禮貌地聆聽。

all.en: Mom served three kinds of tea and a tray of small cinnamon biscuits she had baked since dawn.
all.zh: 媽媽端出三種茶和一盤她從清晨就開始烤的小肉桂餅乾。

all.en: Dad played soft jazz on the record player, with the speakers carefully pointed toward the open garden door.
all.zh: 爸爸用唱盤機放著柔和的爵士樂，把喇叭仔細地對著敞開的花園門。

all.en: Two neighbors asked if the family might host similar gatherings every season throughout the coming year.
all.zh: 有兩位鄰居問這家人未來一年是否能每個季節都舉辦類似的聚會。

all.en: Sarah immediately suggested an autumn lantern walk, and everyone around the table nodded with bright excitement.
all.zh: 莎拉立刻提議辦一場秋季燈籠漫步，桌邊的每個人都興奮地點頭附和。

all.en: Tom volunteered to design the route, while Mom offered to coordinate the baking among interested neighbors.
all.zh: 湯姆自願設計路線，媽媽則自願在有興趣的鄰居之間協調烘焙的分工。

all.en: By sunset, the garden was empty again, but the Lantern Room glowed warmly through the upstairs windows.
all.zh: 日落時花園再次空了下來，但燈籠房透過樓上窗戶仍溫暖地閃耀。

all.en: Mom gently closed her notebook, knowing the cluttered attic had quietly become the cornerstone of their community.
all.zh: 媽媽輕輕闔上筆記本，明白這座曾經雜亂的閣樓如今已悄悄成為他們社區的核心。

all.en: The following weekend, Tom climbed up to the attic and discovered that a sparrow had built a tiny nest right above the skylight.
all.zh: 下個週末，湯姆爬上閣樓，發現一隻麻雀在天窗正上方築了一個小小的巢。

all.en: Sarah refused to let anyone disturb the nest and quietly painted three watercolor portraits of the mother bird that week.
all.zh: 莎拉不准任何人去打擾鳥巢，那週還靜靜地畫了三張母鳥的水彩肖像。

all.en: Dad reluctantly admitted that he had been sneaking up to the attic late at night just to read in complete silence.
all.zh: 爸爸不情願地承認，他一直在半夜偷偷溜上閣樓，只為了能在絕對的安靜中看書。

all.en: Mom started leaving small handwritten notes inside random books, so whoever opened them next would find a tiny surprise.
all.zh: 媽媽開始在隨機幾本書裡夾手寫小紙條，讓下一個翻開那本書的人能收到一個小小驚喜。

all.en: Tom found one note that read remember to call your grandmother on Sunday, and he actually picked up the phone that very afternoon.
all.zh: 湯姆找到一張寫著「星期天記得打電話給奶奶」的小紙條，當天下午他真的拿起電話打過去。

all.en: A neighbor's cat slipped into the attic through the open window and refused to leave the soft cream-colored rug for three whole hours.
all.zh: 一隻鄰居的貓從敞開的窗戶溜進閣樓，整整賴在那張米白色軟地毯上三個小時不肯走。

all.en: Sarah painted that cat too, and the neighbor framed the small portrait as a thank-you gift the very next morning.
all.zh: 莎拉也把那隻貓畫了下來，隔天早上鄰居把這幅小肖像裱框，當作回禮。

all.en: On a rainy Tuesday, Mom hosted an impromptu attic gathering for three neighbors who had been feeling lonely that week.
all.zh: 某個下雨的星期二，媽媽臨時在閣樓辦了一場小聚會，邀請了三位那週覺得孤單的鄰居。

all.en: They drank ginger tea, listened to one of Dad's jazz records, and stayed until the rain finally softened into a quiet drizzle.
all.zh: 他們喝著薑茶，聽著一張爸爸的爵士唱片，一直待到雨終於變成輕柔的毛毛雨。

all.en: Before going to bed that night, Tom added a new line to his story draft: a real home is wherever lonely afternoons can find a chair.
all.zh: 那晚睡前，湯姆在他的故事草稿裡加了一句話：真正的家，是讓孤單的午後能找到一張椅子的地方。
